ArgBinding.description: How a value may arrive from the argument vector.

arity.description: How many values the flag consumes: 0 for a boolean whose presence is the value, 1 otherwise. Derived from the type and written out explicitly so no consumer has to re-derive it.

repeatable.description: Whether the flag may be repeated, accumulating values.

short.description: The short form, without its dash.

value_name.description: The placeholder shown in help for the value.

EnvBinding.description: How a value may arrive from the environment.

aliases.description: Other names still honoured, so a legacy name is recognised rather than reported as a mistake.

description: A declared, machine-readable contract for a program's invocation surface: its argument vector and its environment.

contract_version.description: Major version of this contract format.

generated.description: Emission timestamp (RFC 3339, UTC). Stamped by the emitter.

items.description: One input a program accepts, and the doors it can arrive through.

allowed.description: Accepted tokens; meaningful for the enum and list kinds.

arg.description: How the value may arrive from the argument vector.

default.description: Value used when nothing supplies the input, in its natural JSON form.

deprecation.description: Present only when stability is deprecated: when, what replaces it, and how to migrate.

env.description: How the value may arrive from the environment.

group.description: Free-form grouping tag for documentation and help output.

key.description: Transport-free identity of the input, snake_case. The join key across every source and every generated binding.

maps_to.description: A namespaced key in another configuration surface this input bridges to.

modified.description: Date the declaration was last edited. Stamped by tooling from version control, never hand-written.

observe.description: A field in the program's resolved-configuration output that this input visibly changes; the hook for confirming a declaration empirically.

required.description: Whether the program requires this input.

reviewed.description: Date a human last verified this entry. Hand-written: it asserts human judgement that cannot be derived.

separators.description: Characters that separate items in a list value, so a consumer can split it.

since.description: Version in which the input was introduced.

source.description: Project and revision this record came from; populated when records from several documents are merged.

stability.description: How much a consumer may rely on this input.

summary.description: What the input does, in prose.

type.description: The kind of value. Also fixes how many values the argument form takes: none for bool, one otherwise.

items.title: Input record

precedence.description: Which binding wins when more than one supplies a value, strongest first.

source.description: Emitting project and revision, e.g. "myapp@a1b2c3d". Stamped by the build.

title: ArgEnv contract
